steps.title: Add Buzzsprout embedded audio player to Learn article pages
steps.outline:
Add an optional `buzzsproutEmbedUrl` field to the Article interface and render an embedded Buzzsprout audio player on Learn article pages when the field is present.

The change must:
- Add `buzzsproutEmbedUrl?: string` to the `Article` interface in `lib/learn.ts`
- Create a new server component `components/sections/buzzsprout-player.tsx` that renders a Buzzsprout iframe. The iframe uses the `buzzsproutEmbedUrl` as `src`, width 100%, height 200px, no border, no scrolling
- The component accepts `url: string` and `lang: string` props
- The component renders a heading: "Listen to the Episode" when `lang === 'en'`, "Escucha el Episodio" when `lang === 'es'`
- The component is wrapped in a `ContentSection` with `background="light"`
- In `app/[lang]/learn/[slug]/page.tsx`, render `BuzzsproutPlayer` between the hero section and the first content section only when `article.buzzsproutEmbedUrl` is present and non-empty
- Articles without `buzzsproutEmbedUrl` render identically to before — no layout change
- Do NOT modify any translation dictionary files
- Update `tests/learn.test.ts` to add a test verifying that `buzzsproutEmbedUrl` is present and passed through when included in mock article data

Rough acceptance criteria:
- `Article` interface in `lib/learn.ts` has `buzzsproutEmbedUrl?: string`
- `BuzzsproutPlayer` component exists at `components/sections/buzzsprout-player.tsx`
- Article page renders the player between hero and first content section when `buzzsproutEmbedUrl` is set
- Article page renders no player when `buzzsproutEmbedUrl` is absent
- Player shows correct heading for `en` and `es` lang
- iframe `src` equals the `buzzsproutEmbedUrl` value verbatim
- `pnpm run test` passes including the new test in `tests/learn.test.ts`
- No translation dictionary files are modified

Estimated files: lib/learn.ts, app/[lang]/learn/[slug]/page.tsx, components/sections/buzzsprout-player.tsx (new), tests/learn.test.ts
steps.impl_log:
Done. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

- **`lib/learn.ts`** â€” Added `buzzsproutEmbedUrl?: string` to the `Article` interface.
- **`components/sections/buzzsprout-player.tsx`** â€” New component that renders the section heading ("Listen to the Episode" / "Escucha el Episodio" based on `lang`) and an `<iframe>` with `width="100%"`, `height={200}`, `frameBorder={0}`, `scrolling="no"`.
- **`app/[lang]/learn/[slug]/page.tsx`** â€” Conditionally renders `<BuzzsproutPlayer>` when `article.buzzsproutEmbedUrl` is non-empty.
- **`tests/learn.test.ts`** â€” Added a test asserting `buzzsproutEmbedUrl` round-trips through `getArticle` when present in mock data.

All 20 tests pass (2 test files).

steps.title: Python script to link a published Buzzsprout episode to an article in GCS
steps.outline:
Create a Python script at `scripts/link_buzzsprout_episode.py` that accepts an article slug and a Buzzsprout episode ID, constructs the embed URL, and writes the updated `articles.json` back to the GCS bucket.

The script must:
- Accept `--slug` (article slug string) and `--episode-id` (integer) as required CLI arguments
- Load the following from environment variables (reading `.env` in project root if present via `python-dotenv`): `BUZZSPROUT_PODCAST_ID`, `GCS_BUCKET`, `GCS_ARTICLES_PATH`, `GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS`
- Download `articles.json` from `gs://{GCS_BUCKET}/{GCS_ARTICLES_PATH}` using the `google-cloud-storage` Python library (not gsutil CLI)
- Construct `buzzsproutEmbedUrl` as: `https://www.buzzsprout.com/{BUZZSPROUT_PODCAST_ID}/{episode_id}?client_source=small_player&iframe=true`
- Find the article matching `--slug` and set its `buzzsproutEmbedUrl` field
- If no article matches the slug: exit with code 1 and print a clear error message
- If the article already has `buzzsproutEmbedUrl`: overwrite it and print a warning
- All other articles and all other fields must be preserved exactly
- Upload the updated JSON back to the same GCS path with `content_type='application/json'`
- Pretty-print JSON with `indent=2, ensure_ascii=False`
- Print a success message including the slug and the constructed embed URL

Rough acceptance criteria:
- Script file exists at `scripts/link_buzzsprout_episode.py`
- Script accepts `--slug` and `--episode-id` CLI args
- Script exits with code 1 and error message if slug not found
- Script prints warning if buzzsproutEmbedUrl already exists on target article
- Script constructs embed URL in exact format: `https://www.buzzsprout.com/{BUZZSPROUT_PODCAST_ID}/{episode_id}?client_source=small_player&iframe=true`
- Script uses `google-cloud-storage` library for GCS read/write
- Script uses `python-dotenv` to load `.env` if present
- Only the target article is modified; all other articles and fields are unchanged
- Script prints success message with slug and embed URL on completion

Estimated files: scripts/link_buzzsprout_episode.py (new)
